timeline.label: Q1 2026 net loss $96.7M on revenue $80.9M (+45% YoY); cash $326M after SciTec integration spend
FY2025 revenue $159.9M (+163% YoY); OCF $204.9M; RPO and deferred revenue build on milestone billing
Closed SciTec acquisition — defense software, classified infrastructure, ~$652M purchase price allocation assets
Northrop Grumman partnership on Eclipse medium-lift vehicle; $50M equity investment disclosed
Nasdaq listing (FLY) following IPO / de-SPAC; expanded public float and reporting cadence
Blue Ghost lunar lander program advances; NASA CLPS and national security mission pipeline cited in 10-K
Spaceflight Inc. combination adds on-orbit services capability (disclosed in 10-K segment history)
Alpha rocket achieves orbit — company cites only U.S. small-lift provider with demonstrated orbit in 1,000 kg class

industry.structure: U.S. national-security-led launch and space services oligopoly forming among a handful of qualified primes and new-space entrants; customer concentration toward NASA, Space Force, and prime contractors.
industry.share_shift: Firefly gaining share in small-lift and lunar delivery via Alpha and Blue Ghost wins; medium-lift share depends on Eclipse delivery versus ULA, SpaceX, and Rocket Lab.
industry.trend: Shift to firm-fixed-price defense contracts, higher launch cadence demand, and integrated launch-plus-spacecraft-plus-software stacks (SciTec acquisition).
